rag_rules: When fixing type mismatches in AGREE contracts, ensure left and right sides of binary expressions have matching types.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
generic_rules: AGREE type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid scalar type names.
In AGREE contracts, guarantees must relate visible inputs to visible outputs; avoid implementation-only paths.
Assignments in implementation annexes must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the input model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new data types or features in AGREE annexes that are not already declared in the AADL model.
Do not write guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type annexes for output dependency contracts.
